consent.banner: Just a mirror, not a verdict. There's no right answer, and nothing is stored or tied to you.
consent.footer: For curiosity, not diagnosis. You landed where you landed today, and every side has a flip. Come back tomorrow and you might sit somewhere else.

meta.title: Which Side Are You On?
meta.intro: Nine sayings you already know, each against its opposite. No middle to hide in, you pick a side on every one. At the end you get your map. Then you can flip any of it.

questions.prompt: There's a spare minute. Your hand is on your phone before you decide anything.
You see everyone else's weekend and your own plans suddenly feel small.

options.label: I remember I've got it good

questions.prompt: A friend gets the new thing. Part of you starts doing the math on getting yours.

options.label: Good for them, not my race

questions.prompt: The big thing you keep meaning to start. When does it actually begin?

options.label: I push it more than I'd like

questions.prompt: Something's wrong and you know it. Do you look at it, or scroll past?
A real chance shows up, but taking it means risking what you've already got.

options.label: The only loss is the shot I don't take

questions.prompt: You're not okay. Who actually knows it?
questions.poleB: Wear your heart on your sleeve

options.label: I hold most of it in
The people close to me know

questions.prompt: When you love something, how much of yourself do you give it?
The people you choose to be around, mostly they...
questions.poleA: Birds of a feather flock together

results.sub: Here's where you landed on all nine. The mirrored icons are the sides you flipped to.
results.balancedNote: You split it right down the middle. Rare. You carry both sides at once.
